concerns.text: Task T023 implements a FATAL EXCEPTION if no 'aggregate population data' source is defined, directly violating FR-008 which mandates a fallback mechanism to calculate 'Global Exposure' in this exact scenario. The task effectively disables the FR-008 requirement by refusing to proceed when the fallback condition is met, creating a logical deadlock where the spec requires action but the task forbids it.

Task T036 hardcodes a default threshold of 0.8 (80%) when the config value is '[deferred]', contradicting SC-004. SC-004 states the pipeline must proceed with a warning if the rate is below '[deferred]', implying the threshold is unknown and the check should be lenient or skipped. Hardcoding 0.8 silently relaxes the 'deferred' state into a strict requirement, altering the acceptance criteria.

Task T012a requires a fatal exception if an external 'agent' fails to verify the AMT source. The spec (Section 5) and plan do not define such an external verification agent as a runtime dependency. This introduces a silent constitution drift by adding a hard external dependency not present in the FRs/SCs, risking pipeline failure on infrastructure not specified in the design.

Task T062 mandates removing fallback URLs if a verified source is injected, which contradicts FR-008. FR-008 requires a fallback mechanism (Global Exposure) if birth year data is missing. T062's constraint to 'remove any other fallback URLs' eliminates the possibility of implementing the FR-008 fallback logic, effectively weakening the requirement to handle missing birth years.
